issue: Android Spotify app may surface Google Cast instead of native Spotify Connect in mixed Google Cast homes.
current_assessment: The risk looks real, recent, and largely app-level. The strongest evidence says it mainly affects speakers that expose both Google Cast and Spotify Connect on Android. Non-Cast speakers therefore look materially safer, even when they are still Wi-Fi speakers with native Spotify Connect.

evidence.summary: Spotify moderator Dessi wrote that when Android sees more than one Cast-enabled device, Google Cast becomes the only option in order to enable dynamic grouping.
applies_to: all Google Cast-capable speakers on Android

evidence.summary: A user reported that Bang & Olufsen Beosound A9 5th Gen and Beosound Emerge showed both Spotify Connect and Chromecast on Windows, but only Chromecast in the Android app.
Another user said unplugging a Google Home Mini restored Spotify Connect on Android; plugging it back in removed the Connect option again.
A user reported that disconnecting a Google Audio device made Spotify Connect reappear on Android.

A user with Naim Cast-capable devices said Android only showed Google Cast when more than one Cast device was powered on, and explicitly noted that their previous Bluesound Node had no such problem because it did not support Google Cast.
Repeated user requests complain that Bluesound still has no Chromecast support, which indirectly makes Bluesound less exposed to the Android Cast-vs-Connect failure mode.
Community responders describe Sonos as supporting Spotify Connect and AirPlay but not Chromecast.

model_implications.reason: The specific Android failure pattern mainly affects speakers that expose both Google Cast and Spotify Connect. Sonos community evidence says Sonos is not a Chromecast target, so this speaker looks structurally safer.

Official product material reviewed did not show Google Cast, and Bluesound community posts complain about Chromecast being absent. That is a feature gap for some buyers, but it reduces this exact Android failure mode.
Officially supports Google Cast and has direct recent community evidence showing B&O Emerge and A9 only exposing Chromecast on Android.
No Google Cast support was found in the official docs reviewed, so the exact failure mode looks less likely, but the evidence base is thinner than for Sonos or Bluesound.
Official JBL material clearly advertises Chromecast built-in and Spotify Connect. That puts it in the exposed class even before considering the missing exact Spotify page.
Official Audio Pro material confirms Google Cast, so it sits in the same exposed class as other dual-path Cast-plus-Connect speakers.
